name: Firsdown
overview: Firsdown is a small village and civil parish on the chalk downs about 4.6 miles north-east of Salisbury in Wiltshire, sitting at around 100-130m above sea level and adjoining the larger village of Winterslow. It had a population of around 581 at the 2011 census and lies within the Winterslow and Upper Bourne Valley ward. Largely a 20th-century residential village strung along Firs Road, it shares many services with neighbouring Winterslow, where the parish council meets.
character: A quiet, elevated downland residential village close to Winterslow, valued for its rural calm and easy reach of Salisbury.
amenities: Adjoins Winterslow, which provides shops, pub and village hall
Firsdown has its own parish council (meeting at Winterslow Village Hall)
Gigabit-capable broadband available in parts of the village

schools.name: Winterslow Primary School / Pitton CofE Primary (nearest)
St Joseph's Catholic School / Salisbury grammars (Bishop Wordsworth's, South Wilts)

prices.summary: Predominantly 20th-century houses and bungalows on the downs; more affordable than the premium Nadder/Wylye villages while keeping a rural feel near Salisbury. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks on the surrounding chalk downland
Use of Winterslow's amenities next door
Easy trips to Salisbury and Old Sarum
placesToEat: Pub and eateries in neighbouring Winterslow
pros: Quiet, elevated downland setting close to Salisbury
Shares amenities with the larger village of Winterslow
Reasonable value for the Salisbury area
cons: Few amenities within Firsdown itself (relies on Winterslow)
No railway station; rely on Salisbury
Mostly modern housing with limited period character
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting an affordable, quiet downland village within easy reach of Salisbury, who are happy to use neighbouring Winterslow's amenities.
primarySupermarket: Shops in Winterslow; supermarkets in Salisbury
